ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, indicating no significant depressive symptoms. You appear to be coping well with everyday stressors, and your emotional functioning is healthy.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle with regular exercise and balanced nutrition
Keep up regular social activities and nurture close relationships
Practice preventive mental health habits such as adequate sleep and stress management
Occasional mood fluctuations are a normal part of life

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild depressive symptoms that may be affecting your daily life. At this level, you may notice reduced energy, changes in sleep or appetite, or a general sense of sadness that comes and goes.
suggestions: Pay attention to your emotional well-being and track mood patterns
Maintain regular sleep, exercise, and social routines
Try talking to a trusted friend or family member about how you feel
Consider journaling or mindfulness practices to process emotions
If symptoms persist for more than two weeks, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate depressive symptoms that may be significantly affecting your daily functioning. You may experience persistent low mood, difficulty concentrating, social withdrawal, or loss of interest in previously enjoyed activities.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional for a thorough evaluation
Maintain a structured daily routine to provide stability
Engage in regular physical activity, which has strong evidence for reducing depression
Avoid isolation; stay connected with supportive people in your life
Consider evidence-based psychotherapy such as cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe depressive symptoms that are likely significantly impairing your daily functioning, relationships, and quality of life. Professional help is strongly recommended at this level.
suggestions: Seek consultation with a mental health professional as soon as possible
If you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Do not stay alone; inform a trusted person about your situation
Professional treatment, including therapy and/or medication, can be very effective
Focus on basic self-care: sleep, nutrition, and gentle physical activity

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, suggesting no significant depressive symptoms. Your emotional well-being appears stable, which is positive for overall health in later life.
suggestions: Continue engaging in social and physical activities that you enjoy
Maintain regular routines, hobbies, and meaningful social connections
Stay connected with family and friends to maintain emotional well-being
Regular physical activity, even short walks, supports both mental and physical health

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild depressive symptoms that may be affecting your quality of life. In older adults, depression can sometimes present as fatigue, social withdrawal, or physical complaints rather than overt sadness.
suggestions: Talk to your doctor about how you have been feeling; depression is common and treatable at any age
Stay physically active; even short daily walks can significantly improve mood
Maintain social connections and engage in enjoyable activities
Consider joining a senior community group, class, or volunteer activity

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate depressive symptoms that warrant professional attention. These symptoms may be interfering with daily activities, social engagement, and overall quality of life.
suggestions: Consult your healthcare provider for a comprehensive evaluation
Psychotherapy and/or medication can be effective at any age; do not hesitate to explore options
Try to maintain daily routines and regular sleep patterns
Accept support from family and friends; letting others help is a strength, not a weakness
Be aware that some medications or medical conditions can contribute to depressive symptoms

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe depressive symptoms that are likely significantly affecting your daily life, independence, and well-being. Depression in older adults is very treatable, and seeking help is crucial.
suggestions: Seek medical attention from your healthcare provider as soon as possible
Depression in older adults is very treatable; do not assume it is just part of aging
If you have thoughts of self-harm, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Inform a family member or trusted person about your feelings
A combination of therapy and medication is often the most effective approach

ranges.label: Likely to Be Well
ranges.description: Your score suggests you are likely to be well with no significant psychological distress. Your current level of emotional functioning appears healthy and adaptive.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current healthy coping strategies
Keep up regular physical activity and social engagement
Practice stress management techniques as a preventive measure

ranges.label: Mild Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates mild levels of psychological distress. You may be experiencing some emotional difficulties such as occasional worry, low mood, or restlessness that are beginning to affect daily comfort.
suggestions: Monitor your emotional state over the coming weeks
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, or mindfulness
Talk to a trusted friend or family member about how you feel
Maintain regular sleep, exercise, and nutrition routines
Consider consulting a healthcare professional if symptoms persist or worsen

ranges.label: Moderate Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate levels of psychological distress that may be affecting your daily functioning. At this level, symptoms may include persistent worry, low energy, difficulty concentrating, or sleep problems.
suggestions: Consider seeking help from a mental health professional for evaluation
Maintain regular routines for sleep, exercise, and nutrition
Reduce stressors where possible and prioritize self-care activities
Avoid using alcohol or drugs to cope with distress
Evidence-based therapies such as CBT can be very helpful at this level

ranges.label: Severe Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe psychological distress, which may indicate the presence of a serious mental health condition. This level of distress typically involves significant impairment in daily functioning and quality of life.
suggestions: Please consult a mental health professional or your doctor as soon as possible
If you are in crisis, contact a crisis helpline (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Do not hesitate to reach out to trusted people in your life for support
Follow through with professional treatment recommendations
Remember that severe distress is treatable; many people recover with appropriate help

ranges.label: No or Low Distress
ranges.description: Your score suggests minimal psychological distress. You appear to be functioning well emotionally, and your current coping strategies are effective for managing everyday stressors.
suggestions: Continue your current healthy habits and coping strategies
Maintain regular physical activity and social connections
Practice stress prevention techniques such as adequate sleep and relaxation

ranges.label: Moderate Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate psychological distress that may be affecting your daily comfort and functioning. You may be experiencing symptoms such as nervousness, hopelessness, restlessness, or fatigue that interfere with your usual activities.
suggestions: Pay attention to patterns in your emotional state and identify potential triggers
Practice evidence-based stress reduction techniques such as mindfulness meditation or deep breathing
Maintain social connections and consider talking to someone you trust about your feelings
Consider consulting a mental health professional for evaluation and support

ranges.label: Severe Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe psychological distress that is likely significantly impairing your daily functioning. This level of distress is associated with a high probability of having a diagnosable mental health condition and warrants professional attention.
suggestions: Seek consultation with a mental health professional as soon as possible
If you are in crisis or having thoughts of self-harm, contact a crisis helpline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Reach out to trusted family members or friends for support
Professional treatment including therapy and/or medication can be very effective
Focus on basic self-care while seeking help: adequate sleep, nutrition, and gentle activity
